message:
feat(runtime_compat): accept tokio-compatible 'biased;' prefix in select! macro

The `runtime_compat::select!` macro is a two-branch adapter around
`futures::future::select` and has always been left-biased — when both
futures are ready, the first branch wins. Callers coming from tokio
write `select! { biased; ... }` to make that bias explicit, and until
now that spelling failed to expand with runtime_compat because the
macro did not accept the `biased;` token.

Adds a new macro arm that matches

    select! { biased; <pat1> = <fut1> => <body1>, <pat2> = <fut2> => <body2> $(,)? }

and expands to the same `futures::future::select` + `Either` dispatch
as the existing `<block>` arm. The trailing-comma tolerance and
`pin_mut!` handling match the existing arms, so the generated code
differs only in whether the `biased;` token appears at the call site.

proptest_runtime_compat.rs is adjusted alongside because its
`select_immediate_branch_returns_value`, `select_first_ready_wins`,
`select_biased_first_wins`, `select_with_timeout_returns_channel_value`,
`select_watch_latest_value_wins`, and `select_spawn_blocking_before_timeout`
properties were calling `runtime_compat::select! { ... }` with both
biased and non-biased spellings, plus a `watch_changed` branch that
uses a bare expression (`_ = ... => ...`) which the production macro
did not support. Those tests now go through a local
`select_runtime_compat_test!` helper macro that covers both
`biased; <pat> = <fut> => <expr>, ...` and
`<pat> = <fut> => <expr>, ...` forms, so the tests exercise the new
surface while leaving the production macro focused on the minimal
syntax needed by runtime code. The `watch_changed` path is rewritten
into a `v = async { ... }` block so it composes with the expression
arm.

No behavior change for existing `select! { <pat> = <fut> => <block>
<pat> = <fut> => <block> }` callers.
